channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: US consortium takes over Sheffield Wednesday
item.description: Sheffield Wednesday have announced that US consortium Arise Capital Partners has completed a takeover of the club.

item.title: All but safe? Leeds ease 'anxiety' - and increase pressure on others
item.description: No club has ever been relegated with 43 points at the end of a Premier League season - so have Leeds already done enough to secure survival?

item.title: 'Shy' Anderson on England nerves and becoming a leader
item.description: Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson tells Kelly Somers about coming out of his shell to become a leader.

item.title: PSG and Bayern in 'different world' to Premier League teams - Arteta
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says Champions League rivals Paris St-Germain and Bayern Munich are in a "different world" to tired Premier League teams.

item.title: The striker quietly making a big noise at Arsenal
item.description: As Arsenal travel to Lyon in the Champions League semi-final second leg, BBC Sport assesses the importance of unsung striker Stina Blackstenius.

item.title: Leeds beat Burnley to move closer to safety
item.description: Leeds move nine points clear of the relegation places with a home victory over managerless Burnley.

item.title: 'It's like we're all crying and relegated' - Spurs' De Zerbi delivers monologue
item.description: Manager Roberto de Zerbi urges Tottenham to "silence the voice inside of us" as the club battle to avoid relegation from the Premier League.

item.title: How WSL 2 title contenders Charlton are defying the odds
item.description: Hardly anyone was talking about Charlton being WSL 2 title contenders before the season began - but they go into the final day top.

item.title: Saudi Arabian owners' desire for Newcastle 'unchanged'
item.description: Head coach Eddie Howe says Newcastle's owners remain "ambitious" for the club despite the Saudi Arabian PIF's decision to pull out of funding LIV Golf.

item.title: Joao Pedro on Barca radar - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Barcelona eye Chelsea's Joao Pedro and Tottenham's Cristian Romero, Aston Villa want Paris St-Germain's Ibrahim Mbaye, Leeds United and Coventry City battle for Lois Openda, plus more.

item.title: Time for Isak & Wirtz to prove they are the future for Liverpool
item.description: Without Mohamed Salah, Liverpool need others to take responsibility and the focus is set to intensify on Alexander Isak and Florian Wirtz.

item.title: Forest winger fined for failing to tax supercar
item.description: Callum Hudson-Odoi is ordered to pay more than £1,100 after he failed to tax his McLaren supercar.

item.title: Tuchel helped me understand new role - Anderson
item.description: Nottingham Forest midfielder Elliot Anderson praises Thomas Tuchel for helping him develop in a deeper midfield role.

item.title: Infantino attempts to orchestrate Israel-Palestine handshake
item.description: Fifa president Gianni Infantino tries to convince Palestinian and Israeli representatives to shake hands at the Fifa Congress in Vancouver.

item.title: Leeds held nerve in 'massive' win - Farke
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says his side "held their nerve" in their "massive" 3-1 victory over Burnley in the Premier League.

item.title: Difficult 24 hours no excuse for Burnley errors - interim boss Jackson
item.description: Burnley's interim manager Mike Jackson says the difficult 24 hours after Scott Parker left the club is no excuse for some of the errors made in his side's 3-1 defeat to Leeds United in the Premier League.

item.title: 'Too much to handle' - Bright on social media abuse
item.description: Millie Bright sits down with the BBC's Jo Currie to talk about her decision to retire from football.

item.title: Why was Mudryk given ban in secret and what happens now?
item.description: Chelsea winger Mykhailo Mudryk is appealing against a four-year doping ban but why has the process been kept secret, and what happens now?

item.title: Hypnosis, Al Pacino and lies - how I tried to motivate my players
item.description: Tony Pulis explains some of the unorthodox ways he tried to motivate players during his 30 years as a manager, including how some of them did not work.

item.title: What does future hold for Havertz after season blighted by injury?
item.description: Arsenal have largely been unable to call upon Kai Havertz in a season blighted by injury, so what does the future hold for the Germany forward?

item.title: SPFL will not overrule Celtic in derby ticket row
item.description: The SPFL has backed Celtic in a row over the allocation of away tickets for this month's final Old Firm meeting of the season with Rangers.

item.title: 'We got police escort to Vardy's party' - Leicester's 5,000-1 title win 10 years on
item.description: Premier League title-winning captain Wes Morgan and former Leicester City team-mate Marc Albrighton reflect on the club's 5,000-1 triumph a decade on.

item.title: 'I proved I am still a capable manager' - Hodgson
item.description: Bristol City interim boss Roy Hodgson says he has proved to himself he can still succeed as a manager at 78.

item.title: Arteta 'fuming' at overturned Eze penalty
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta believes that the penalty awarded against Atletico Madrid for a foul on Eberechi Eze was not a "clear and obvious error", meaning VAR did not need to intervene and the original on-field decision to give the penalty should have stood.

item.title: Inspiring being part of Arsenal squad - Blackstenius
item.description: Forward Stina Blackstenius talks about Arsenal's Women's Champions League hopes and explains why she has extended her stay with the club.

item.title: Arsenal hit seven to seal Leicester play-off fate
item.description: Arsenal close the gap on leaders Manchester City to eight points with two games in hand, after a 7-0 win over Leicester City - who will finish bottom of the Women's Super League.

item.title: Parker's final post-match interview as Burnley boss
item.description: Burnley head coach Scott Parker says his side must "come back stronger" after their 1-0 defeat to Manchester City meant they were relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: All I want to do is help as much as I can - Grealish
item.description: England midfielder Jack Grealish speaks to Joe Wilson about extending his Special Olympics GB ambassador role and what it means to him.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v Ella and Jake from Jamie Johnson FC
item.description: Chris Sutton takes on Ella and Jake from BBC football drama Jamie Johnson FC plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's fixtures.

item.title: Illegal streaming gang ordered to repay £3.75m
item.description: Mark Gould, the driving force behind the illegal streaming service Flawless TV, faces an added 10 years in prison if he fails to pay his share within three months.

item.title: Crues retain Premiership status with shootout win over Annagh
item.description: Crusaders secured their Irish Premiership status for next season after a dramatic penalty shootout victory which sealed the play-off victory over Annagh United.

item.title: Northern Ireland to play Guinea in June friendly
item.description: Northern Ireland will play Guinea on Thursday, 4 June in an international friendly in Spain.

item.title: 'Where are you? Wow. It is so, so bad' - Emery's impassioned VAR rant
item.description: Aston Villa boss Unai Emery says VAR made a "huge mistake" in not sending off Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson in their Europa League semi-final first leg.

item.title: 'If Kane scored that we'd be saying wow' - Strand Larsen shows Palace worth
item.description: Being a club's record signing brings with it pressure, but Jorgen Strand Larsen puts a difficult start behind him with a goal that could be crucial in Crystal Palace's pursuit of a first major European trophy.

item.title: McInnes wants Tynecastle 'in full glory' for Rangers game
item.description: Hearts boss Derek McInnes says he wants to see Tynecastle in "its full glory" on Monday night when Rangers arrive in the capital.

item.title: Hardest thing is knowing you're no longer at your best - Rooney
item.description: Former England and Manchester United striker Wayne Rooney believes the most difficult thing as a footballer is knowing you are no longer the player you were.

item.title: Hibs fan views won't affect Celtic game - O'Neill
item.description: Martin O'Neill can "understand" why some Hibs fans may not want Hearts to win the league, but does not expect such sentiment to affect Celtic's trip to Easter Road.

item.title: The mind and methods of Wrexham's composed icon
item.description: Those who have worked under Phil Parkinson reveal the secrets behind his astonishing achievements with Wrexham as they chase another play-off finale.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - McTominay v Griffiths
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Who needs what in promotion and play-off race on Championship final day
item.description: Get up to speed on the race for automatic promotion and the play-off places ahead of what should be a dramatic final day of the Championship season.

item.title: Pedersen poised for Wednesday new era to begin
item.description: Sheffield Wednesday manager Henrik Pedersen is confident the takeover to launch a bright new era for the Owls will finally be completed.

item.title: 'I lost myself' - Bright on retirement, abuse and the future
item.description: Former England and Chelsea captain Millie Bright speaks to BBC Sport a day after announcing her retirement.

item.title: Making history and facing Neymar - Lingard on life in Brazil
item.description: In his first major interview since moving to Brazil, Jesse Lingard talks to the BBC about his new life, and Manchester United.

item.title: How 10 Premier League teams can qualify for Europe
item.description: Ten Premier League teams in Europe? It sounds preposterous, but it is not as far-fetched as it seems.

item.title: Why Rashford must be patient over Barcelona future
item.description: Marcus Rashford is not ready to give up on his Barcelona dream just yet, writes Spanish football expert Guillem Balague.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: World Cup 2026 injury watch - key names racing to be ready
item.description: After Spain's Lamine Yamal was ruled out of the rest of his club season, BBC Sport looks at other key names racing to be fit for the World Cup - and those who are set to miss out through injury.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: English clubs pursue Rangers' Rice - gossip
item.description: Rangers midfielder linked with English clubs as Celtic players face operations.

item.title: Watch the goals as Livingston draw with Aberdeen
item.description: Watch the goals as Livingston twice come back to draw with Aberdeen in the Scottish Premiership (UK users only).

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Three into two - battle to reach WSL goes to final day
item.description: Just one matchday remains in the WSL 2 and Birmingham City, Crystal Palace and Charlton Athletic could all secure automatic promotion.

item.title: Leicester City women face relegation play-off - what happened?
item.description: Leicester City Women have been confirmed in a relegation play-off with the team that finished third in WSL 2, so what might that mean for the club?

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
